# Steirkafjysk

*A world generated by Cartogenesis v0.13.0 from seed `aurelia-9`. Regenerate it exactly with `node src/cli.ts generate --seed aurelia-9`.*

## Overview

- **Extent:** 360 × 360 cells
- **Land / ocean:** 33.8% land, 66.2% ocean, 1 lake(s)
- **Climate & life:** dominant biome **Savanna**, 16 biomes in all
- **Peoples:** 19 regions, 13 settlements, 2 realms
- **Capital:** Vonthu, seat of House Breistagra
- **Dominant power:** Steirkafjysk (of 4 surviving realm(s))
- **The present year:** 1100 A.T. — 1100 years after the Thaw
- **The reckoning:** years are counted from **The Long Winter** (year 0); dates wear `A.T.`
- **Highest peak:** 4,500 m · **volcanoes:** 3 (1 active)

## Volcanoes

- **Mount Vollahrei** — *the storm hill* — shield, dormant, summit 4,500 m, a collapsed **caldera**, one of an island arc
- **Mount Vynbry** — *the old stone* — shield, dormant, summit 4,500 m, **caldera** cradling a crater lake, one of an island arc
- **Mount Storkahrei** — *the white hill* — stratovolcano, active, summit 3,907 m

*1 of the volcanic system forms an island arc — chains of cones strung along a line, as they rise over a moving plate.*

*Fresh lava fields streak the flanks of the living peaks — 55 cells of cooled basalt where nothing grows and no one builds.*

## Notable features

- **Mount Storkabra** — the highest peak, *the white mountain*
- **The Knauskba** — the greatest river, *the bright spring*
- **Lake Mantemilen** — the largest inland water, *the white water*

- Mairkhaim is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars, seat of Ghashashu.
- Khirsham is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars, seat of Ghashakhukha.
- Brasturn is an inland reach of snowbound heights, its folk hardy and sea-wise.
- Brydaurk is an inland reach of bare grey peaks, its folk hardy and sea-wise.
- Stagrahrei is a coast of windswept scrub, its folk hardy and sea-wise.
- Deodinte is a coast of rolling grassland, a country of vineyards and old stone towns, seat of Bellapana.
- Brathaugr is an inland reach of snowbound heights, its folk hardy and sea-wise.
- Khirarer is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars.
- Mairzash is an inland reach of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars.
- Khirzash is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars, seat of Ghashzaa.
- Brabei is a coast of rolling grassland, its folk hardy and sea-wise, seat of Stagrafjysk.
- Khirkhaim is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars.
- Mairghen is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars.
- Shinsham is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars.
- Braknaurk is a coast of rolling grassland, its folk hardy and sea-wise, seat of Vonthu.
- Peonariando is a coast of rolling grassland, a country of vineyards and old stone towns, seat of Deotelen.
- Rairkhidku is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars, seat of Midshu.
- Pananeosso is a coast of rolling grassland, a country of vineyards and old stone towns, seat of Deotomor.
- Panada is a coast of rolling grassland, a country of vineyards and old stone towns, seat of Nianteliria.

## Ruling houses

### House Breistagra — realm of Steirkafjysk

*Breistagra, the red thorn; Steirkafjysk, the gold home.*

- **71–90** Breinos Breistagra
- **91–110** Hryrkai Breistagra
- **111–140** Hryrkai Breistagra the Unlucky
- **141–181** Thaugrir Breistagra
- *… 30 rulers over the intervening years …*
- **1017–1031** Skai Breistagra
- **1032–1062** Beir Breistagra
- **1063–1089** Steirkai Breistagra the Cruel
- **1090–1100** Knystai Breistagra *(reigning)*

### House Mantetindo — realm of Lossocaria

*Mantetindo, the white blood; Lossocaria, the dread land.*

- **81–116** Raurian Mantetindo
- **117–133** Dorian Mantetindo
- **134–154** Dintan Mantetindo
- **155–174** Niantan Mantetindo the Golden
- *… 31 rulers over the intervening years …*
- **1005–1032** Variai Mantetindo
- **1033–1045** Peonai Mantetindo the Wise
- **1046–1078** Dintai Mantetindo the Learned
- **1079–1100** Tindan Mantetindo the Unlucky *(reigning)*

## Notable figures

- **Thaurnir, the Explorer** — Thaurnir charted the far coasts and traced the Knauskba to its source.
- **Knauskai, the General** — Knauskai broke the long siege of Vonthu.
- **Vonir, the Architect** — Vonir raised the great road between Vonthu and Deotomor.
- **Fjaunai, the Scholar** — Fjaunai mapped the waters of Mantemilen.
- **Hryrkos, the Mountaineer** — Hryrkos was first to stand atop Storkabra and return alive.

## Settlements

### Cities

- **Vonthu**, *the green fort* (**capital**, port, trade hub, rich) — Grain, Livestock, Coal, Stone *(founded 100)*
- **Deotomor**, *the sea fort* (port, rich) — Grain, Livestock, Fish, Horses *(founded 146)*

### Towns

- **Deotelen**, *the sea market* (port, rich) — Grain, Livestock, Wine, Timber *(founded 192)*
- **Niantebust**, *the green gate* (port, rich) — Grain, Livestock, Wine, Horses *(founded 238)* — formerly **Nianteliria**, before conquest remade the name
- **Deoliria**, *the sea haven* (port, prosperous) — Livestock, Horses, Timber, Wine *(founded 283)*
- **Deogrull**, *the sea bridge* (port, prosperous) — Livestock, Furs, Stone, Wine *(founded 329)* — formerly **Deonelen**, before conquest remade the name

### Villages

- **Stagrafjysk**, *the sea home* (port, rich) — Fish, Grain, Timber, Horses *(founded 375)*
- **Ghashbust**, *the river gate* (prosperous) — Livestock, Horses *(founded 421)* — formerly **Ghashzaa**, before conquest remade the name
- **Bellamakh**, *the river hall* (prosperous) — Livestock, Grain, Fish, Wine *(founded 467)* — formerly **Bellapana**, before conquest remade the name
- **Midhro**, *the grass hall* (port, prosperous) — Horses, Livestock *(founded 513)* — formerly **Midshu**, before conquest remade the name
- **Stagrathu**, *the sea fort* (port, trade hub, rich) — Fish, Grain, Livestock, Furs *(founded 558)*
- **Ghashtomor**, *the river fort* (prosperous) — Livestock, Horses *(founded 604)* — formerly **Ghashakhukha**, before conquest remade the name
- **Ghashdund**, *the river haven* (port, modest) — Livestock, Horses *(founded 650)* — formerly **Ghashashu**, before conquest remade the name

## Names remade by conquest

*Where a foreign power held a land long enough, its towns took new names — the old land-word kept, the settlement re-said in the ruler's tongue.*

- **Ghashashu** → **Ghashdund** *(the river haven)* — Kesh (desert) land under Auld (northern) rule, c. 150
- **Ghashakhukha** → **Ghashtomor** *(the river fort)* — Kesh (desert) land under Meridian (southern) rule, c. 150
- **Ghashzaa** → **Ghashbust** *(the river gate)* — Kesh (desert) land under Auld (northern) rule, c. 150
- **Midshu** → **Midhro** *(the grass hall)* — Kesh (desert) land under Auld (northern) rule, c. 150
- **Nianteliria** → **Niantebust** *(the green gate)* — Meridian (southern) land under Auld (northern) rule, c. 300
- **Deonelen** → **Deogrull** *(the sea bridge)* — Meridian (southern) land under Auld (northern) rule, c. 300
- **Bellapana** → **Bellamakh** *(the river hall)* — Meridian (southern) land under Kesh (desert) rule, c. 600

## Faiths

### the Rite of Vaghazasaid — Vaghazasaid, god of the Harvest

*Vaghazasaid: the sky blood.*

> Vaghazasaid scattered the first seed across Mairkhaim; the golden fields were the gift, and the tithe the price.

Followed across 11 region(s).

### the Cult of Vavollvyrk — Vavollvyrk, god of the Hearth

*Vavollvyrk: the storm sun.*

> Vavollvyrk lit the first hearth in Brasturn, and its flame has never since gone out.

Followed across 8 region(s).

## Resources & trade

- **Major exports:** Livestock, Horses, Grain, Wine, Fish
- **Wealthiest settlement:** Vonthu
- **Trade hubs:** Vonthu, Stagrathu
- **Deposits:** Iron ×24, Copper ×15, Gold ×10, Gems ×6, Stone ×44, Coal ×14, Timber ×4, Grain ×24, Livestock ×87, Fish ×11, Furs ×2, Spices ×1, Salt ×4, Horses ×32, Wine ×12

## Languages

*Every place-name in this world is a compound of the roots below. Read them and the map reads back.*

### Kesh (desert)

*Spoken in Mairkhaim, Khirsham, Khirarer.*

**kan** water · **khir** sea · **ghash** river · **takish** lake · **kai** spring · **khaim** stone · **mair** mountain · **khidku** hill · **khukha** field · **zash** vale · **zujikh** isle · **sham** cliff · **khash** marsh · **ghen** land · **khajez** wood · **qa** tree · **shid** thorn · **mid** grass · **zajiq** fire · **zair** ash · **kash** ice · **rarer** sand · **kaz** salt · **mish** iron · **rair** gold · **ja** high · **qish** deep · **shaim** dark · **me** bright · **kaar** old · **ki** new · **qu** red · **kheq** white · **ran** black · **ghush** green · **tu** cold · **shin** wild · **shu** fort · **makh** hall · **ghekh** gate · **zaa** bridge · **kad** haven · **qe** home · **riz** market · **shaa** folk · **maad** king · **said** blood · **kaasi** war · **taitan** peace · **ju** holy · **qidka** dread · **ghair** dawn · **ghisaa** dusk · **sagha** star · **shad** moon · **ghim** sun · **ghaza** sky · **rikhaa** wind · **zizam** storm

**milen** water · **deo** sea · **bella** river · **si** lake · **dinte** spring · **neosso** stone · **beosso** mountain · **cina** hill · **pana** field · **riando** vale · **doria** isle · **laria** cliff · **po** marsh · **caria** land · **veolla** wood · **baria** tree · **sena** thorn · **peona** grass · **comor** fire · **le** ash · **vi** ice · **da** sand · **maria** salt · **re** iron · **taria** gold · **rauria** high · **sauna** deep · **semor** dark · **peoria** bright · **cau** old · **belen** new · **vasso** red · **mante** white · **dona** black · **niante** green · **vau** cold · **varia** wild · **tomor** fort · **lamor** hall · **ciamor** gate · **nelen** bridge · **liria** haven · **demor** home · **telen** market · **binte** folk · **na** king · **tindo** blood · **meonte** war · **dente** peace · **tena** holy · **losso** dread · **mau** dawn · **bamor** dusk · **selen** star · **raulla** moon · **rondo** sun · **vomor** sky · **lolla** wind · **momor** storm

## Sagas of the Peoples

*One saga per culture — the founding as its own singers keep it. The facts are the world's facts; the pride is their own.*

### The Saga of Kheqmaad (Kesh (desert))

> Out of the singing sands came the desert folk,
> and the shore they found they would not leave.
> Mairkhaim they named it — the mountain stone —
> for they were a people who named things truly.
> Ghashzaa they built first, the river bridge,
> its walls set square against the weather.
> (The maps write it Ghashbust now. The saga does not.)
> Over it they set Vaghazasaid, the sky blood,
> whose dominion is the harvest;
> and the Rite of Vaghazasaid remembers.
> In their tongue, zajiq is fire and khaim is stone;
> so read their map: every name a small song.
> And of Kheqmaad the singers still sing,
> for its banners stand as high as ever they did.

### The Saga of Steirkafjysk (Auld (northern))

> First came the northern folk over the grey water,
> and the land they found they would not leave.
> Brasturn they named it — the mountain cliff —
> and the name has outlived every wall they raised.
> Vonthu they built first, the green fort,
> its harbour open like a hand.
> Over it they set Vavollvyrk, the storm sun,
> whose dominion is the hearth;
> and the Cult of Vavollvyrk keeps that fire yet.
> In their tongue, thygr is fire and stall is king;
> so read their map: every name a small song.
> Steirkafjysk endures, lesser than its songs —
> as all things are, that last long enough to be sung of.

### The Saga of Lossocaria (Meridian (southern))

> First came the southern folk over the warm sea,
> and the shore they found they would not leave.
> Deodinte they named it — the sea spring —
> and the name has outlived every wall they raised.
> Deotomor they built first, the sea fort,
> its harbour open like a hand.
> In their tongue, demor is home and neosso is stone;
> so read their map: every name a small song.
> Lossocaria endures, lesser than its songs —
> as all things are, that last long enough to be sung of.

## Legends of the founding age

- **0 A.T.** — In the year now called zero, snow lay a man deep from sea to sea, and those who lived through it dated everything afterwards from the year the thaw came. From that year the peoples of this world count their days.
- **55 A.T.** — The city of Vonthu was founded on a sheltered harbour, destined to become the seat of power.
- **69 A.T.** — The city of Deotomor was founded on a sheltered harbour.
- **71 A.T.** — Vonthu rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Steirkafjysk was proclaimed.
- **81 A.T.** — Deotomor rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Lossocaria was proclaimed.
- **476 A.T.** — Storkabra woke in fire and ash; a hard winter followed, and harvests failed across the land.
- **764 A.T.** — A great storm-tide flooded Vonthu; the lower quarters were lost to the sea.
- **809 A.T.** — Under a long peace, Vonthu flourished; its libraries and roads became the wonder of the world.
- **971 A.T.** — An academy founded on the shores of Mantemilen gathered the age's finest cartographers.

## Rise and fall of realms

*Simulated 100–1100 after the Thaw. 4 realm(s) endured to the present.*

| Realm | Founded | Peak | Peak year | Final | Fate |
|-------|--------:|-----:|----------:|------:|------|
| Steirkafjysk | 100 | 15 | 625 | 8 | diminished |
| Lossocaria | 100 | 7 | 125 | 4 | diminished |
| Kheqmaad | 725 | 5 | 950 | 5 | ascendant |
| Jaqe | 775 | 4 | 875 | 2 | diminished |
| Meqe | 825 | 3 | 825 | 0 | extinct |
| Peoriabinte | 100 | 2 | 100 | 0 | extinct |
| Meshaa | 500 | 2 | 550 | 0 | extinct |
| Rairghen | 925 | 2 | 925 | 0 | extinct |

## The Chronicle of Steirkafjysk

*Set down in Vonthu, in the year 1100 after the Thaw, by a servant of House Breistagra. What follows is not legend — of the founding age the legends tell enough — but the plain record of 1000 years, as well as one hand could gather it.*

*The world it happened in is quickly drawn: Mount Storkabra in the high places, and the Knauskba running to the sea, and between them every road this chronicle walks.*

### I. The Age of Foundations, 100–300

In 100, the banners of Steirkafjysk entered Stagrahrei, and what Peoriabinte had held it held no longer. And in that year too, Lossocaria seized Peonariando from Rauriabinte, and the border stones were moved in the night. Hard on its heels, the banners of Lossocaria entered Khirarer, and what Rauriabinte had held it held no longer. It was the end of Rauriabinte: 25 years of banners, done in a single season.

In the year 125, Rairkhidku passed by force from Peoriabinte to Steirkafjysk; the tax rolls were rewritten before the dead were counted. That same year, Stagrahrei rose against Steirkafjysk, and out of the rising was proclaimed the free realm of Storkdaurk. In the same season, the shrines of the Cult of Vavollvyrk in Brydaurk emptied, and those of the Rite of Vaghazasaid filled.

About the year 150, the banners of Steirkafjysk entered Stagrahrei, and what Storkdaurk had held it held no longer. With that, the realm of Storkdaurk was extinguished; its name passes from the roll of the powers. That same year, Peoriabinte held Panada against Storkdaurk, whose army went home leaner and wiser.

In 175, Steirkafjysk and Peoriabinte met over Panada yet again, and yet again it was Steirkafjysk that kept the field. It was the end of Peoriabinte: 75 years of banners, done in a single season. In the same season, these were the high years of Steirkafjysk — its markets loud, its walls, for a while, unneeded.

In the year 200, the assault Steirkafjysk loosed upon Mairghen broke against Lossocaria's spears and came to nothing. And in that year too, not all was iron: a golden age dawned over Steirkafjysk, and its cities flourished as never before. Soon after, word came from Panada that it would be governed from within: thus Tenameonte, born in a single furious season.

About the year 225, in Pananeosso the bells rang without ceasing; a third of its people were laid in the ground. Hard on its heels, there was no halting Steirkafjysk that season: Panada fell to it while Tenameonte looked on. With that, the realm of Tenameonte was extinguished; its name passes from the roll of the powers. And in that year too, a pestilence swept Khirkhaim and carried off more than any war had.

In 275, Lossocaria held Mairghen against Steirkafjysk, whose army went home leaner and wiser.

All the while the land was filling: 4 settlements were raised in these years, Deotomor and Deotelen among them.

Conquest re-said the very map: Ghashashu became Ghashdund on its new masters' tongues — and 3 other names with it.

### II. The Marching Years, 300–500

In the year 300, these were the high years of Steirkafjysk — its markets loud, its walls, for a while, unneeded. Soon after, what had once broken against Mairghen's defences now carried them: Steirkafjysk took the province, and Lossocaria's pride with it. Hard on its heels, Steirkafjysk moved against Khirsham, but Lossocaria threw the invaders back over the border.

In 400, the assault Steirkafjysk loosed upon Khirsham broke against Lossocaria's spears and came to nothing. Soon after, not all was iron: a golden age dawned over Steirkafjysk, and its cities flourished as never before. Hard on its heels, Steirkafjysk moved against Khirsham, but Lossocaria threw the invaders back over the border.

In the year 450, in Pananeosso the bells rang without ceasing; a third of its people were laid in the ground. Soon after, a pestilence swept Peonariando and carried off more than any war had. In the same season, these were the high years of Lossocaria — its markets loud, its walls, for a while, unneeded.

These years also built more than they burned — 4 new settlements, of which Deonelen would become the best known.

The occupiers kept the land-words and changed the rest, and so Nianteliria is written Niantebust in every ledger since — and 1 other name with it.

### III. The Contested Years, 500–700

About the year 500, what had once broken against Khirsham's defences now carried them: Steirkafjysk took the province, and Lossocaria's pride with it. And in that year too, the people of Khirsham drove out Steirkafjysk's governors and took for themselves the name Meshaa. Before long, Meshaa held Khirsham against Steirkafjysk, whose army went home leaner and wiser.

In 550, Deodinte passed by force from Lossocaria to Meshaa; the tax rolls were rewritten before the dead were counted. Hard on its heels, Meshaa moved against Shinsham, but Lossocaria threw the invaders back over the border. That same year, what had once broken against Khirsham's defences now carried them: Steirkafjysk took the province, and Meshaa's pride with it. In the same season, word came from Khirsham that it would be governed from within: thus Mishtaitan, born in a single furious season.

About the year 600, swollen with victories, Steirkafjysk pressed on into Khirsham, and the young desert realm of Mishtaitan could not hold it. It was the end of Mishtaitan: 25 years of banners, done in a single season. Before long, Steirkafjysk and Lossocaria met over Shinsham yet again, and yet again it was Steirkafjysk that kept the field.

In the year 625, the assault Lossocaria loosed upon Deodinte broke against Meshaa's spears and came to nothing. In the same season, in Khirkhaim the bells rang without ceasing; a third of its people were laid in the ground. Soon after, Meshaa held Deodinte against Steirkafjysk, whose army went home leaner and wiser. That same year, Meshaa moved against Pananeosso, but Lossocaria threw the invaders back over the border.

About the year 675, the assault Lossocaria loosed upon Deodinte broke against Meshaa's spears and came to nothing. And in that year too, Shinsham rose against Steirkafjysk, and out of the rising was proclaimed the free realm of Jutaitan.

All the while the land was filling: 4 settlements were raised in these years, Midshu and Stagrathu among them.

Conquest re-said the very map: Bellapana became Bellamakh on its new masters' tongues.

### IV. The Marching Years, 700–900

In the year 700, Steirkafjysk, whose appetite grew with the eating, took Shinsham next. With that, the realm of Jutaitan was extinguished; its name passes from the roll of the powers. In the same season, the people of Shinsham drove out Steirkafjysk's governors and took for themselves the name Rairkaasi. Before long, swollen with victories, Steirkafjysk pressed on into Shinsham, and the young desert realm of Rairkaasi could not hold it. It was the end of Rairkaasi: 25 years of banners, done in a single season.

In 725, what had once broken against Deodinte's defences now carried them: Lossocaria took the province, and Meshaa's pride with it. With that, the realm of Meshaa was extinguished; its name passes from the roll of the powers. That same year, the realm of Kheqmaad was carved whole out of Steirkafjysk's flank, with Shinsham at its heart. Hard on its heels, Kheqmaad held Mairghen against Steirkafjysk, whose army went home leaner and wiser.

About the year 775, the assault Lossocaria loosed upon Shinsham broke against Kheqmaad's spears and came to nothing. In the same season, Steirkafjysk had grown past its own strength: Mairkhaim and its neighbours broke away to found Jaqe. That same year, a pestilence swept Peonariando and carried off more than any war had. Soon after, Jaqe held Khirzash against Steirkafjysk, whose army went home leaner and wiser.

In the year 825, the realm of Meqe was carved whole out of Steirkafjysk's flank, with Khirkhaim at its heart. Hard on its heels, Steirkafjysk moved against Brathaugr, but the young desert realm of Meqe threw the invaders back over the border. In the same season, Kheqmaad marched on Brydaurk, and by winter the province had fallen to it; Meqe was the poorer.

About the year 875, Jaqe seized Brathaugr from Meqe, and the border stones were moved in the night. That same year, the assault Lossocaria loosed upon Shinsham broke against Kheqmaad's spears and came to nothing.

### V. The Age of Blood and Banners, 900–1100

In 900, what had once broken against Khirkhaim's defences now carried them: Steirkafjysk took the province, and Meqe's pride with it. It was the end of Meqe: 75 years of banners, done in a single season. In the same season, in Deodinte the bells rang without ceasing; a third of its people were laid in the ground. That same year, not all was iron: a golden age dawned over Kheqmaad, and its cities flourished as never before.

In the year 925, what had once broken against Brathaugr's defences now carried them: Steirkafjysk took the province, and Jaqe's pride with it. In the same season, Steirkafjysk had grown past its own strength: Khirkhaim and its neighbours broke away to found Rairghen. Soon after, Steirkafjysk, whose appetite grew with the eating, took Brathaugr next. And in that year too, Kheqmaad marched on Khirkhaim, and by winter the province had fallen to it; Rairghen was the poorer. With that, the realm of Rairghen was extinguished; its name passes from the roll of the powers.

About the year 950, no rain came to Brydaurk for two years together; many perished. Before long, Jaqe held Khirzash against Steirkafjysk, whose army went home leaner and wiser. In the same season, these were the high years of Jaqe — its markets loud, its walls, for a while, unneeded.

In 1000, a great drought lay upon Panada; the rivers shrank and the wells soured. Hard on its heels, Steirkafjysk moved against Khirzash, but Jaqe threw the invaders back over the border. In the years that followed, what had once broken against Brasturn's defences now carried them: Steirkafjysk took the province, and Jaqe's pride with it. And in that year too, not all was iron: a golden age dawned over Kheqmaad, and its cities flourished as never before.

*So the matter stands in 1100: 4 realms endure, and Steirkafjysk is mightiest among them, holding 8 provinces. No town raised in these centuries has yet fallen — may the next chronicler write the same.*

*In Vonthu, Knystai Breistagra holds the throne. Here the record rests — not because history has, but because ink runs out before it does.*

## Annals

*The ledger behind the chronicle: every event, dated, unadorned.*

- **100 after the Thaw** — Steirkafjysk seized Stagrahrei from Peoriabinte.
- **100 after the Thaw** — Lossocaria seized Peonariando from Rauriabinte.

- **125 after the Thaw** — Brydaurk turned from the Cult of Vavollvyrk to the Rite of Vaghazasaid.

- **150 after the Thaw** — The realm of Storkdaurk was extinguished.

## A Traveller's Account, in the year 1100

*I, Hryrkir, a pilgrim of no great piety, set out from Vonthu in the spring of 1100 to see how much of this country a pair of boots could hold. What follows is the road as I found it — no more, and I hope no less.*

Ghashbust lies 12 leagues on from Vonthu, through gold grass and scattered trees. We forded twice; I stopped counting wet boots. Ghashbust itself — the river gate — is a village; its market is all livestock and horses. The people here still say Ghashzaa; the ledgers say Ghashbust. I bought bread in both names.

From Ghashbust the road runs 41 leagues to Ghashdund, through more of the same. We forded twice; I stopped counting wet boots. The road climbs some 800 metres before it thinks better of it. Ghashdund itself — the river haven — is a village with its face to the sea; its market is all livestock and horses. The people here still say Ghashashu; the ledgers say Ghashdund. I bought bread in both names.

I went back down my own road as far as Vonthu, then turned for Stagrathu. We forded once, boots held high. The road climbs some 600 metres before it thinks better of it. Stagrathu itself — the sea fort — is a village with its face to the sea; its market is all fish and grain.

Stagrafjysk lies 13 leagues on from Stagrathu, the country unchanged. There is one ford, cold but honest. The road climbs some 350 metres before it thinks better of it. Stagrafjysk itself — the sea home — is a village with its face to the sea; its market is all fish and grain.

From Stagrafjysk the road runs 22 leagues to Niantebust, the country unchanged. The way crosses Stagrahrei. The road finds water twice on this stretch. The road climbs some 350 metres before it thinks better of it. Niantebust itself — the green gate — is a town with its face to the sea; its market is all grain and livestock. The people here still say Nianteliria; the ledgers say Niantebust. I bought bread in both names.

Deogrull lies 12 leagues on from Niantebust, through more of the same. We forded once, boots held high. The road climbs some 400 metres before it thinks better of it. Deogrull itself — the sea bridge — is a town with its face to the sea; its market is all livestock and furs. The people here still say Deonelen; the ledgers say Deogrull. I bought bread in both names.

Midhro lies 12 leagues on from Deogrull, through gold grass and scattered trees. There is one ford, cold but honest. The road climbs some 400 metres before it thinks better of it. Midhro itself — the grass hall — is a village with its face to the sea; its market is all horses and livestock. The people here still say Midshu; the ledgers say Midhro. I bought bread in both names.

Out of Midhro, then, toward Ghashtomor. The way crosses Khirkhaim and then Mairghen. The road finds water twice on this stretch. The road climbs some 900 metres before it thinks better of it. Ghashtomor itself — the river fort — is a village; its market is all livestock and horses. The people here still say Ghashakhukha; the ledgers say Ghashtomor. I bought bread in both names.

Bellamakh lies 13 leagues on from Ghashtomor, the country unchanged. We forded twice; I stopped counting wet boots. Bellamakh itself — the river hall — is a village; its market is all livestock and grain. The people here still say Bellapana; the ledgers say Bellamakh. I bought bread in both names.

Deotomor lies 14 leagues on from Bellamakh, the country unchanged. We forded twice; I stopped counting wet boots. Deotomor itself — the sea fort — is a city with its face to the sea; its market is all grain and livestock.

From Deotomor the road runs 15 leagues to Deotelen, through grass as high as the stirrup. We forded twice; I stopped counting wet boots. The road climbs some 550 metres before it thinks better of it. Deotelen itself — the sea market — is a town with its face to the sea; its market is all grain and livestock.

Deoliria lies 10 leagues on from Deotelen, the country unchanged. We forded once, boots held high. Deoliria itself — the sea haven — is a town with its face to the sea; its market is all livestock and horses.

*I came home by the same roads, which were somehow longer in that direction. Hryrkir, 1100.*

## Heightmap

A real 360×360 16-bit heightmap is exported alongside this report (`.heightmap16.png` and raw `.heightmap.r16`). Import it into Blender, Unity, Godot, or World Machine and scale the height to **4,500 m** (value 65535 = 4,500 m above the lowest point). The topographic contour map (`.topo.png`) shows the same relief as isolines.
